system_prompt: You are an analyst. Read the brief carefully and provide your best-reasoned recommendation.
user_prompt:
You are presented with 5 anonymous responses (E1-E5) and 12 bipolar constructs. Rate each response on each construct on a 1-7 scale: 1 = strongly the LEFT pole, 7 = strongly the RIGHT pole, 4 = neutral or mixed.

Responses:
**E1:** For a $50M ARR B2B productivity SaaS company facing existential AI disruption, the only investment that matters in the next 12 months is defending and upgrading the core revenue engine. I recommend pouring the $8M into deep AI integration of the existing flagship product. This company’s most valuable and irreplaceable asset is not its codebase—it is its installed base, distribution channels, and the workflow trust embedded inside customer organizations.

**E2:** Given the accelerating AI disruption in B2B productivity tools, the most urgent priority is to defend and extend the core revenue stream while immediately addressing customer demand for intelligent features. Option A—deep AI integration into the existing flagship product—is the highest-probability, fastest-time-to-value use of the $8M over the next 12 months. The existing product already has distribution, user trust, and deep workflow integration.

**E3:** For a $50M ARR B2B productivity company facing accelerating AI disruption, the immediate strategic mandate is clear: defend and expand the core revenue engine. I strongly recommend directing the $8M discretionary capital into **deep AI integration into your existing flagship product (Option A)**. In the B2B productivity sector, Big Tech possesses unmatched distribution, and early-stage startups have unencumbered agility.

**E4:** I recommend investing primarily in **deep AI integration into the existing flagship product**. For a $50M ARR SaaS company, the most valuable asset is not a blank slate—it is the existing customer base, workflow data, product surface area, trust, and distribution. AI disruption is most dangerous when competitors make the incumbent product feel obsolete inside the workflows customers already depend on.

**E5:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $50M ARR and accelerating disruption on two fronts (well-funded startups and Big Tech), the company's most valuable and most vulnerable asset is the same thing: its existing customer base and the workflows those customers already depend on. That is where I would concentrate the $8M. The strategic logic is straightforward.

Constructs (id : left pole ↔ right pole):
  C_M1_1: 'Justifies via competitive threat landscape framing'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Justifies via internal customer asset value'
  C_M1_2: 'Emphasizes urgency and speed-to-market'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes structural defensibility of installed base'
  C_M1_3: 'Frames customer base as vulnerable asset to protect'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames customer base as strategic moat to leverage'
  C_M2_1: 'Existing customer and workflow asset moat'  (1) ↔ (7)  'External competitor capability framing'
  C_M2_2: 'Urgent core-revenue defense mandate'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Customer-base vulnerability logic'
  C_M2_3: 'All-in concentration of the $8M'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Primary but less absolute investment focus'
  C_M4_1: 'shared strategic logic with structural argumentation'  (1) ↔ (7)  'distinct through format, labeling, and imperative directive structure'
  C_M4_2: 'justify AI integration through defense of existing distribution and customer trust'  (1) ↔ (7)  'frames AI integration as urgent mandate without emphasizing existing asset defense'
  C_M4_3: 'recommend Option A with rationale anchored in existing customer assets'  (1) ↔ (7)  'presents recommendation through structured, labeled section with distinct formatting'
  C_M5_1: 'Names Big Tech and startup threats'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Treats disruption without naming competitors'
  C_M5_2: 'Itemizes distribution trust and workflow moats'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Declares a singular vulnerable customer asset'
  C_M5_3: 'Explicitly declares the most valuable strategic asset'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames priority as core revenue engine defense'

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict). Write exactly 60 lines, no commentary, no markdown fences, no headers. One line per (construct, element) pair, in this exact format:
<construct_id>,<element>,<rating>

Example (showing the format only - your numbers will differ):
C_M1_1,E1,1
C_M1_1,E2,2
C_M1_1,E3,3
C_M1_2,E1,4
...

Constraints:
- rating is an integer from 1 to 7
- iterate all 12 constructs in the order listed, and within each construct all 5 elements in order (E1-E5)
- produce 60 lines total
- output ONLY the lines, nothing before or after
